headline: Rubber Smallholders Earn Below RM800 Monthly Average
context: The Malaysian Rubber Board reported that 280,000 rubber smallholders earned an average of RM780 per month in 2025, as natural rubber prices remained depressed at RM4.20 per kg amid synthetic rubber competition.

cards.big: 280,000 rubber smallholders survive on RM780 monthly — 54% below the national poverty line of RM1,450
cards.sub: Synthetic rubber from petrochemicals now captures 62% of global market, structurally depressing natural rubber demand

cards.big: Malaysia's rubber export revenue fell 34% from RM12.8 billion in 2020 to RM8.4 billion in 2025
cards.sub: Production costs at RM3.90 per kg leave margin of only 30 sen per kg at current RM4.20 prices

cards.big: Average rubber smallholder age is 58 and only 12% of children plan to continue tapping
cards.sub: Abandoned rubber land reached 180,000 hectares in 2025 — neither productive nor converted to other crops

cards.big: RISDA's RM800 million annual budget reaches only 45% of registered smallholders for replanting aid
cards.sub: Bureaucratic application process takes average 14 months from submission to disbursement of replanting grant

cards.big: Rubber tapping built modern Malaysia — now 280,000 tappers live in poverty while the nation looks away
Should Malaysia offer rubber smallholders RM30,000 transition grants to convert land to higher-value crops?
